title: Electronic Warfare
description: RAND's research on electronic warfare examines the technologies and strategies militaries use to manage and defend the electromagnetic spectrum—the signals that enable communication, radar, and sensing. It provides insights to help defense planners strengthen protection against jamming and other electronic threats, improving resilience and effectiveness in conflict environments.
text:
Assessing the Practical Feasibility of the Clader-Jacobs-Sprouse Quantum Algorithm for Calculating Radar Cross Sections
The authors of this report examine the Clader-Jacobs-Sprouse quantum computing algorithm's potential practical utility for modeling a target's radar cross section.
Wartime innovation and adaptation: Supporting Ukraine’s digital transformation
A series of reports on how to optimize Kyiv’s technological and policy advantages as it defends its sovereignty.
Can Technological Adaptation Maintain Ukraine's Battlefield Edge?
In this webinar, RAND Europe researchers reflect on Ukraine’s pioneering use of technology in wartime and share their insights into this technological innovation.
Electromagnetic Warfare: NATO's Blind Spot Could Decide the Next Conflict
The war in Ukraine has revealed a critical weakness in NATO's defenses: electromagnetic warfare (EW). While Russia dominates this invisible battlespace, Ukraine is learning in combat what the West neglected in training. To close the gap, NATO must quickly build its own EW capabilities or risk falling behind.
Can technological adaptation maintain Ukraine's battlefield edge?
In this webinar, RAND Europe researchers reflect on Ukraine's pioneering use of technology and share their insight into this technological innovation.
Dispersed, Disguised, and Degradable: The Implications of the Fighting in Ukraine for Future U.S.-Involved Conflicts
Current conflicts offer insights into how future wars might be fought. This report describes eight novel or notable observations about the Russia-Ukraine war and summarizes their relevance to hypothetical U.S.-involved wars with near-peer opponents.
Lessons from the War in Ukraine for Space: Challenges and Opportunities for Future Conflicts
The role of space-based services in the war in Ukraine offers important lessons for preparing for events in the space domain. RAND researchers recount the war’s space activities, examine three mission areas, and extract relevant lessons.
The Expansion of China's Military Space and Counterspace Capabilities and Implications for Space as a Contested Domain: Addendum
Document submitted April 23, 2025, as an addendum to testimony before the U.S.-China Economic and Security Review Commission on April 3, 2025.
Small Uncrewed Aircraft Systems (SUAS) in Divisional Brigades: Electronic Warfare and Spectrum Management Considerations
The authors assessed challenges and opportunities related to integrating small uncrewed aircraft systems with electronic warfare capabilities in divisional brigades and identified ways to make XVIII Airborne Corps units more effective in meeting spectrum management challenges.
Small Uncrewed Aircraft Systems (SUAS) in Divisional Brigades: Small UAS and Counter-UAS Training
The authors identified options for the Army to field and employ small uncrewed aircraft systems at the division, BCT and battalion levels to enhance fire support capabilities.
The Expansion of China's Military Space and Counterspace Capabilities and Implications for Space as a Contested Domain
Testimony presented before the U.S.-China Economic and Security Review Commission on April 3, 2025.
Back to the Future of Comms
Outgunned by Russia in electronic warfare, Kyiv should consider going further than it already has with more cost-effective communication solutions. Sometimes vintage systems and techniques can provide militaries with simpler and cheaper alternatives to keep critical lines of communication open.
